library.name: Basic Scene Grammar
library.description: Core scene-based grammar library for high-frequency structures, contrastive function words, and sentence frames.

items.title: because + clause
items.coreExplanation: 用 because + 从句 直接说明原因；because 后面必须接“主语+谓语”的完整句子。
items.usageNote: 口语和写作都常用；在正式写作中可用 since/as 更委婉，because 更直接。

slotSchema.label: Reason clause
slotSchema.hint: 完整从句：主语+谓语（如：I was tired / it was raining）

slotSchema.label: Main clause (result)
slotSchema.hint: 结果/主句

commonErrors: because 后面只接名词或短语（应改为 because of + 名词/动名词，或补全从句）
because 后面用不完整从句（缺主语或谓语）
把 because 和 so 放在同一句里造成重复（英语里通常二选一）

contrasts.title: because of + noun / v-ing
contrasts.note: because of 后面接名词/动名词短语；because 后面接完整从句。

contrasts.title: since + clause (reason)
contrasts.note: since 更委婉、偏书面或逻辑提示；because 更强调直接原因。

contrasts.title: because of + noun / v-ing
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: since + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: as + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: I stayed home because it was raining.
examples.translation: 我待在家里，因为正在下雨。
examples.note: because 后接完整从句 it was raining。

examples.sentence: Because I was tired, I went to bed early.
examples.translation: 因为我累了，所以我早早就睡了。
examples.note: because 从句也可放句首，后面通常用逗号隔开。

templates.label: Basic
slotHints: 先说结果/决定，再用 because 说明原因
reasonClause 用完整句：主语+动词
templates.exampleSentence: We canceled the picnic because the weather was bad.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们取消了野餐，因为天气不好。

items.title: because of + noun / v-ing
items.coreExplanation: “because of”后面接名词/名词短语或动名词（v-ing）来表示原因；不接完整从句。
items.usageNote: 常放在句首或句中；句首多用逗号隔开。

slotSchema.label: Reason (noun / v-ing)
slotSchema.hint: 名词/名词短语或v-ing，不要用“主语+谓语”从句

commonErrors: 误用：because of + S + V（应改为 because + 从句 或把原因改成名词/动名词）
because of 后用不定式 to do（多数情况应改为 v-ing 或名词）
句首原因短语后不加逗号导致读起来不清晰

contrasts.title: because + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: due to + noun
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: Because of the heavy rain, the game was canceled.
examples.translation: 因为大雨，比赛被取消了。
examples.note: 句首用逗号分隔，原因是名词短语。

examples.sentence: We stayed home because of feeling sick.
examples.translation: 因为觉得不舒服，我们待在家里。
examples.note: because of + v-ing（feeling）。更口语也可说：because we felt sick。

templates.label: Basic
slotHints: reason：名词/名词短语或v-ing
templates.exampleSentence: Because of traffic, I was late.
templates.exampleTranslation: 因为堵车，我迟到了。

items.title: since + clause (reason)
items.coreExplanation: 用“since + 从句”引出原因，语气更像“既然/鉴于”，把原因当作双方已知或默认的背景信息，后面接结论或决定。
items.usageNote: 多用于书面或较正式的说明、邮件；口语强调直接原因时更常用because。

slotSchema.label: Reason clause
slotSchema.hint: 陈述一个已知/默认的原因（一般不用疑问句）

slotSchema.label: Main clause (result/decision)
slotSchema.hint: 说明结论、安排或建议

commonErrors: 把since误解为“自从”（时间含义）导致语义不清
用since引出明显新信息或强烈因果时不自然（应改because）
since后面接名词短语（应为because of/due to）

contrasts.title: because + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: as + clause (reason)
contrasts.note: as语气更弱、更书面；since常带“既然/鉴于”的前提感。

contrasts.title: as + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: Since you’ve already finished, you can leave early.
examples.translation: 既然你已经做完了，你可以提前走。
examples.note: 原因当作已知事实，语气像“既然”。

examples.sentence: Since the data is incomplete, we can’t draw a conclusion yet.
examples.translation: 鉴于数据不完整，我们还不能下结论。
examples.note: 偏书面说明背景原因。

templates.label: Decision based on a known reason
slotHints: {reason}: 已知/默认的原因从句
{result}: 结论/决定从句
templates.exampleSentence: Since it’s raining, we’ll take a taxi.
templates.exampleTranslation: 既然下雨了，我们就打车。

items.title: as + clause (reason)
items.coreExplanation: 用“as + 从句”表示原因，语气较轻，常把原因当作背景信息；更常见于书面语/叙述中。
items.usageNote: 常放句首并用逗号隔开；不适合强调、回答“为什么”的强因果。

slotSchema.label: as clause（原因从句）
slotSchema.hint: 用一般陈述句；常放句首：As + S + V

slotSchema.label: main clause（主句）
slotSchema.hint: 结果/决定/动作

commonErrors: 用as回答直接提问“Why...?”导致语气太轻（更常用because）
把as当“当……时候”用，造成歧义（as也可表时间/方式）
句首as从句后漏逗号，书面表达不自然

contrasts.title: because + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: since + clause (reason)
contrasts.note: 与as相近，但since常带“既然/既然如此”的前提感。

contrasts.title: since + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: As it was raining, we stayed indoors.
examples.translation: 因为在下雨，我们就待在室内。
examples.note: 原因作背景，句首更自然。

examples.sentence: As you are new here, I’ll show you around.
examples.translation: 既然你刚来，我带你熟悉一下环境。
examples.note: 语气温和，接近since的“既然”。

templates.label: Background reason (as)
slotHints: 填原因陈述：it was..., you are..., we don’t have...
填结果/决定：we..., I’ll..., the meeting was...
templates.exampleSentence: As we don’t have enough data, we’ll postpone the decision.
templates.exampleTranslation: 由于数据不够，我们将推迟决定。

items.title: due to + noun
items.coreExplanation: “due to + 名词/名词短语”表示原因，常用于较正式的说明、报告或公告中，相当于“由于/因为”。通常放在句中或句尾，用来解释结果。
items.usageNote: 更偏正式；口语里更常用“because of”。“due to”后面接名词（如：due to bad weather），不要直接接完整句子。

slotSchema.label: Cause (noun phrase)
slotSchema.hint: 原因用名词/名词短语：bad weather, a system error, staff shortage

slotSchema.label: Result (clause)
slotSchema.hint: 结果用完整句子：the meeting was postponed

commonErrors: due to 后面直接接完整句子（✗ due to it rained）
把 due to 当作 because 使用但结构不完整（✗ due to we were late）
误用 thanks to 表示负面原因（负面更适合 due to / because of）

contrasts.title: because of + noun / v-ing
contrasts.note: 语气更中性/口语；且可接动名词：because of being late。due to 通常接名词短语。

contrasts.title: thanks to + noun
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: because of + noun / v-ing
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: The flight was delayed due to bad weather.
examples.translation: 航班因恶劣天气而延误。
examples.note: 公告/通知常用。

examples.sentence: Due to a system error, the report was not generated.
examples.translation: 由于系统错误，报告未能生成。
examples.note: 更像书面/报告语气；句首后常加逗号。

templates.label: Cause phrase for formal explanation
slotHints: 先写结果，再用 due to 引出原因
cause_np 用名词短语，不要用完整句子
templates.exampleSentence: The event was canceled due to safety concerns.
templates.exampleTranslation: 活动因安全方面的担忧而取消。

items.title: thanks to + noun
items.coreExplanation: “thanks to + 名词/名词短语”表示“多亏……/幸亏……”，强调原因带来好结果，常带有感谢或庆幸的语气。
items.usageNote: 可放句首或句中；后面一般接名词短语（也可接代词）。想接动词时更常用“thanks to + the fact that + 从句”。

slotSchema.label: helpful cause
slotSchema.hint: 人/帮助/支持/好条件等，如 your help, the new system

slotSchema.label: positive result
slotSchema.hint: 积极结果，如 we finished on time

commonErrors: 把“thanks to”用在负面原因上（更适合用 because of / due to）
误写成“thank to”或漏掉s：应为 thanks to
后面直接接动词原形：thanks to help（应为 thanks to your help / thanks to helping…较少见）

contrasts.title: because of + noun / v-ing
contrasts.note: 中性原因，正负都可；不一定带感谢语气。

contrasts.title: due to + noun
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: because of + noun / v-ing
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: Thanks to your help, we finished the project on time.
examples.translation: 多亏你的帮助，我们按时完成了项目。
examples.note: 句首用逗号隔开，更突出原因。

examples.sentence: I got there early thanks to the new subway line.
examples.translation: 多亏新地铁线，我到得很早。
examples.note: 句末也常见，语气更口语。

templates.label: Cause first
slotHints: cause=有利原因（名词短语）
result=积极结果（完整陈述）
templates.exampleSentence: Thanks to the clear instructions, I didn’t make any mistakes.
templates.exampleTranslation: 多亏说明清楚，我没有犯任何错误。

items.title: so + result clause
items.coreExplanation: 用“原因，so + 结果”把前一句当作原因，so引出顺势发生的结果；口语里很常用。
items.usageNote: 常用逗号隔开：cause, so result. 也可分成两句：Cause. So result.

slotSchema.label: Cause clause
slotSchema.hint: 先说原因（完整句）

slotSchema.label: Result clause
slotSchema.hint: so后面接结果（完整句）

commonErrors: 把“so”当成“因为”用：So I was tired...（应为Because...）
和because连用：Because..., so...（一般二选一）
so后面缺主谓，只放一个词导致不完整（写作时尤其明显）

contrasts.title: therefore
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: That's why + result
contrasts.note: That's why强调“这就是原因”，更像解释/总结；so更像顺着讲故事。

contrasts.title: that's why + result
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: I missed the bus, so I took a taxi.
examples.translation: 我错过了公交车，所以我打了个出租车。
examples.note: 自然口语因果。

examples.sentence: It started raining, so we stayed inside.
examples.translation: 开始下雨了，所以我们待在室内。

templates.label: Cause → so → Result
slotHints: cause：一个原因句（过去/现在都可以）
result：一个结果句（常见：决定/行动/状态）
templates.exampleSentence: I was running late, so I called you.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我快迟到了，所以我给你打电话。

items.title: that's why + result
items.coreExplanation: “That’s why + 结果”用来在说完原因后，强调并总结结论/结果（=“所以这就是为什么……”）。常见结构：原因。That’s why + 结果。
items.usageNote: 多用于口语或书面中带总结语气；结果部分通常是一个完整句子或具体行动。

slotSchema.label: result clause
slotSchema.hint: 用完整结果句，常含主语+谓语；也可用建议/决定等行动结果

commonErrors: 把“that’s why”当作“because”用：不要用“That’s why I was tired, I slept late.”（应先原因后结果）
“That’s why”后面只接名词短语：应接结果句（或用“That’s the reason for…”）
原因与结果缺少逻辑对应，听起来像硬总结

contrasts.title: so + result clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: the reason is that + clause
contrasts.note: 用于解释原因本身；“that’s why”用于总结结果/结论。

contrasts.title: the reason is that + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: I missed the last bus. That’s why I’m late.
examples.translation: 我错过了最后一班公交车，所以我才迟到。
examples.note: 先给原因，再用“That’s why”强调结论。

examples.sentence: The data is incomplete. That’s why we can’t publish the report yet.
examples.translation: 数据不完整，所以我们还不能发布报告。

templates.label: Cause. That’s why + result.
slotHints: cause写原因句（可用一般过去/现在完成等）
result写结果句，表达结论/决定/影响
templates.exampleSentence: I didn’t sleep well last night. That’s why I’m so tired today.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我昨晚没睡好，所以我今天特别累。

items.title: although + clause
items.coreExplanation: 用although引导让步从句，表示“尽管……，但是……”。前半句提出不利情况，后半句给出结果/立场，语气较书面、较标准。
items.usageNote: although从句里不再用but/so等连词；也可放句末：Main clause, although + clause。

slotSchema.label: Concession clause
slotSchema.hint: 完整从句（主语+谓语），说明“尽管……”

slotSchema.label: Main clause
slotSchema.hint: 主要结论/结果

commonErrors: 在although从句后又加but（✗ Although..., but ...）
把although后面接名词/动名词（应改用despite/in spite of）
只写从句不写主句，导致意思不完整

contrasts.title: even though + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: despite + noun / v-ing
contrasts.note: 后面接名词或v-ing，不接完整从句：Despite the rain / Despite being tired,...

contrasts.title: despite + noun / v-ing
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: Although the deadline is tight, we can still finish the report on time.
examples.translation: 虽然截止日期很紧，但我们仍然能按时完成报告。
examples.note: 书面表达常用，still加强“仍然”。

examples.sentence: I went for a walk, although it was raining.
examples.translation: 我还是去散步了，尽管当时在下雨。
examples.note: although从句放句末更自然、语气稍轻。

templates.label: Standard
slotHints: concession: 不利情况/让步信息
main: 结果/决定/观点
templates.exampleSentence: Although I was tired, I kept working.
templates.exampleTranslation: 虽然我很累，但我还是继续工作。

items.title: even though + clause
items.coreExplanation: “even though + 从句”表示“尽管/虽然”，用来强调让步与转折更强烈，主句结果依然成立（常带“出乎意料”的对比感）。
items.usageNote: 后面必须接完整从句（有主语+谓语）。可放句首或句中；句首常用逗号分隔。

slotSchema.label: even though 从句
slotSchema.hint: 完整从句：主语+动词，如 even though I was tired

slotSchema.label: 主句
slotSchema.hint: 表达结果/仍然发生的事，如 I kept working

commonErrors: 把 even though 后面接名词/动名词：even though the rain（应为 even though it was raining / despite the rain）
和 even if 混用：even though 表示事实让步；even if 表示假设让步
双重让步：Although/Even though... but...（英语里通常不加 but）

contrasts.title: although + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: even if + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: Even though I was exhausted, I finished the report.
examples.translation: 尽管我筋疲力尽，我还是把报告写完了。
examples.note: 事实让步：确实很累，但结果仍完成。

examples.sentence: She smiled, even though she was nervous.
examples.translation: 她还是笑了，尽管她很紧张。
examples.note: 可放句中，突出情绪对比。

templates.label: Basic
slotHints: concessionClause=一个事实性的让步从句
mainClause=主要结果（仍然发生/仍然成立）
templates.exampleSentence: Even though it was raining, we went for a walk.
templates.exampleTranslation: 尽管在下雨，我们还是去散步了。

items.title: despite + noun / v-ing
items.coreExplanation: despite后面接名词短语或动名词（v-ing），表示“尽管/即使……，仍然……”。不能直接接完整从句。
items.usageNote: 多用于书面或较正式表达；口语也可用但更常见于写作。

slotSchema.label: 让步内容
slotSchema.hint: 名词短语或v-ing短语，如 the rain / being tired

slotSchema.label: 主要结果/动作
slotSchema.hint: 主句，说明结果或继续发生的事

commonErrors: 用despite直接接从句：despite he was tired（应改为 although he was tired 或 despite being tired）
把despite当作连词使用并加逗号连接两句
despite后用to do：despite to work hard（应为 despite working hard）

contrasts.title: although + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: in spite of + noun / v-ing
contrasts.note: 意义与despite几乎相同；in spite of更长、更正式一点。

contrasts.title: in spite of + noun / v-ing
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: Despite the rain, we went hiking.
examples.translation: 尽管下雨，我们还是去徒步了。
examples.note: despite + 名词短语

examples.sentence: Despite being tired, she finished the report on time.
examples.translation: 尽管很累，她还是按时完成了报告。
examples.note: despite + v-ing

templates.label: Basic
slotHints: concession用名词短语或v-ing短语
main写主要结果/动作（完整主句）
templates.exampleSentence: Despite the delay, the meeting started on time.
templates.exampleTranslation: 尽管有延误，会议还是准时开始了。

items.title: in spite of + noun / v-ing
items.coreExplanation: 表示“尽管/虽然”，后面接名词、代词或动名词（v-ing），后面不直接接完整句子。常用于书面表达，语气比 despite 稍长、更正式一点。
items.usageNote: 若要接从句，用 in spite of the fact that + 从句，或改用 although/though + 从句。

slotSchema.label: 让步内容
slotSchema.hint: 名词/代词/动名词短语（v-ing）

slotSchema.label: 主要结果
slotSchema.hint: 主句表达结果或事实

commonErrors: 把从句直接放在 in spite of 后面：in spite of he was tired...（应改为 in spite of the fact that... 或 although...）
把 of 漏掉：in spite the rain（应为 in spite of the rain）
误用 to do：in spite of to be late（应为 in spite of being late）

contrasts.title: despite + noun / v-ing
contrasts.note: 意义几乎相同；despite 更简洁，in spite of 更长一点。

contrasts.title: although + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: despite + noun / v-ing
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: In spite of the rain, we went hiking.
examples.translation: 尽管下雨，我们还是去徒步了。
examples.note: in spite of + 名词短语 + 逗号 + 主句。

examples.sentence: In spite of feeling exhausted, she finished the report on time.
examples.translation: 尽管感到筋疲力尽，她还是按时完成了报告。
examples.note: in spite of + v-ing（动名词短语）。

templates.label: Basic
slotHints: concession: 名词/代词/v-ing
main: 主句结果
templates.exampleSentence: In spite of the delay, the meeting started on time.
templates.exampleTranslation: 尽管有延误，会议还是准时开始了。

items.title: even if + clause
items.coreExplanation: “even if + clause”表示“即使/就算……也……”，强调前面是设想的条件，不会改变主句结果。
items.usageNote: 常用于表达决心、让步或反驳：不管发生什么，结果/决定不变。

slotSchema.label: even if 条件分句
slotSchema.hint: 设想的情况（不一定会发生）

slotSchema.label: 主句结果
slotSchema.hint: 不受条件影响的决定/结果

commonErrors: 把 even if 当成 even though（把假设写成事实）
even if 后面用名词短语：even if the rain（应为 even if it rains / even if there is rain）
从句和主句时态乱用：even if it will rain（更自然：even if it rains / even if it does rain）

contrasts.title: even though + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: if + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: Even if it rains, we’ll go hiking.
examples.translation: 即使下雨，我们也会去徒步。
examples.note: 强调决定不变；“下雨”是设想。

examples.sentence: Even if you don’t agree, I’m going to tell the manager.
examples.translation: 即使你不同意，我也要告诉经理。
examples.note: 用于争论/表态。

templates.label: Basic
slotHints: condition: 一个完整分句（S+V）
result: 主要决定/结果
templates.exampleSentence: Even if I’m busy, I’ll reply tonight.
templates.exampleTranslation: 即使我很忙，我今晚也会回复。

items.title: while + clause (contrast)
items.coreExplanation: 用于把两件事并列对照（而非表示“同时”）。常见结构：While + 子句, 主句。也可倒装为：主句, while + 子句。
items.usageNote: 多用于书面/说明性写作来做对比；口语里也可用，但更常用 whereas/ but。注意：这里的 while=“而、然而”，不是“当……时”。

slotSchema.label: while 从句
slotSchema.hint: 描述A的情况/特征，用一般现在时很常见

slotSchema.label: 主句
slotSchema.hint: 描述与A形成对照的B

commonErrors: 把对比用 while 误解成时间用 while，导致句意跑偏
while 从句后面漏逗号（While..., 主句）或乱加逗号
把 while 后面接名词短语（应是子句；名词用 whereas不行，需改成 although/ despite 等结构）

contrasts.title: however
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: although + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: While the new model is faster, it costs much more.
examples.translation: 新款机型更快，但价格也高得多。
examples.note: 对比两点：速度 vs 价格。

examples.sentence: While some people prefer to work from home, others work better in an office.
examples.translation: 有些人更喜欢在家工作，而另一些人在办公室工作效率更高。

templates.label: contrast (While..., ... )
slotHints: 在这里写A的对照点（完整主谓结构）
在这里写B的对照点（完整主谓结构）
templates.exampleSentence: While the plan sounds simple, it will be hard to execute.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这个计划听起来很简单，但执行起来会很难。

items.title: however (sentence adverb)
items.coreExplanation: However 作句子副词表示“然而/不过”，用来承接上一句并引出对比或转折的新观点，通常放在第二句句首，后面用逗号隔开。
items.usageNote: 多用于书面语/论文/报告；口语更常用 but。不要把 however 当作连词直接连接两个独立句。

slotSchema.label: first statement
slotSchema.hint: 先提出观点/事实A

slotSchema.label: contrasting statement
slotSchema.hint: 提出对比/限制/相反观点B

commonErrors: 把 however 当连词用：*I was tired, however I kept working.（缺少分号/句号）
在 however 后不用逗号（正式写作中建议：However, ...）
与 but 重复使用：*But however, ...

contrasts.title: but
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: while + clause (contrast)
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: The plan seems efficient. However, it may be too costly.
examples.translation: 这个方案看起来很高效。然而，它可能成本太高。
examples.note: 第二句对第一句提出限制/反面信息。

examples.sentence: I agree with your goal. However, I disagree with your method.
examples.translation: 我同意你的目标。然而，我不同意你的方法。

templates.label: Basic contrast across sentences
slotHints: 陈述A
转折/对比陈述B
templates.exampleSentence: The data are promising. However, the sample size is small.
templates.exampleTranslation: 数据很有前景。然而，样本量很小。

items.title: if + present, will / can + base
items.coreExplanation: 用于表达“如果某个现实/很可能发生的条件成立，那么将会/能够做某事”。if从句用一般现在时表将来；主句常用will或can+动词原形。
items.usageNote: 也可把主句放前：I’ll call you if I arrive early. 注意if从句不用will。

slotSchema.label: If clause (present)
slotSchema.hint: 一般现在时：If + 主语 + 动词(原形/三单)…

slotSchema.label: Main clause (will/can + base)
slotSchema.hint: will/can + 动词原形，表达结果/决定/能力

commonErrors: 在if从句里用will（误：If it will rain...）
把if从句用将来时（误：If I will go...）
主句动词忘记用原形（误：will goes / can goes）

contrasts.title: unless + clause, main clause
contrasts.note: unless=“除非/如果不”。结构类似，但语义是否定条件。

contrasts.title: if + past, would + base
contrasts.note: 用于不太可能/假设/非现实情况；语气更委婉或不真实。

contrasts.title: unless + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: if + past, would + base
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: If it rains tomorrow, we’ll stay home.
examples.translation: 如果明天下雨，我们就待在家。
examples.note: if从句一般现在时表达将来可能情况。

examples.sentence: If you finish early, you can leave first.
examples.translation: 如果你提前完成，你可以先走。
examples.note: 主句用can表示允许/可能。

templates.label: Basic
slotHints: 用一般现在时描述条件
用will/can+动词原形描述结果
templates.exampleSentence: If you have time, I’ll show you around.
templates.exampleTranslation: 如果你有时间，我带你四处看看。

items.title: unless + clause
items.coreExplanation: unless + 从句表示“除非/如果不”，相当于“if ... not”，用于提出例外条件。
items.usageNote: 常用于规则、警告、条款。unless 从句一般用一般现在时表达将来；不要再加 not 形成双重否定。

slotSchema.label: unless 从句
slotSchema.hint: 描述“如果不…”的条件，一般用现在时

slotSchema.label: 主句
slotSchema.hint: 说明结果/后果，常用 will/can/must 等

commonErrors: 在 unless 从句里再加 not：unless you don't...（应改为 unless you...）
把 unless 当成“除非…否则…一定…”的强制逻辑，忽略语境（有时更像一般条件）
时态误用：用 will 出现在 unless 从句里（一般用现在时表达将来）

contrasts.title: if + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: in case + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: Unless you wear a helmet, you can't enter the site.
examples.translation: 除非你戴安全帽，否则你不能进入工地。
examples.note: 规则/规定场景常用。

examples.sentence: I won’t go unless you come with me.
examples.translation: 除非你和我一起去，不然我不去。
examples.note: 口语中常用来设条件。

templates.label: Basic rule/warning
slotHints: 用一般现在时描述条件，如 you have a ticket / you wear a helmet
主句写结果，常用 will/can/must/need to
templates.exampleSentence: Unless you have a ticket, you can't get in.
templates.exampleTranslation: 除非你有票，否则你进不去。

items.title: if + past, would + base
items.coreExplanation: 用来谈论“与现在事实相反/不太可能发生”的假设：if从句用一般过去时，主句用would+动词原形。意思常等于“如果……就会……（但现实不是这样）”。
items.usageNote: 口语中would常缩写为’d；be动词在正式/考试中常用were（If I were...）。

slotSchema.label: if-clause (past simple)
slotSchema.hint: 与现在相反的条件：If I had more time / If she lived here

slotSchema.label: main clause (would + base)
slotSchema.hint: 结果：I would travel more / she would help

commonErrors: 把从句写成将来时：If I will...（应为If I had/was/were...）
主句用过去时而不是would：If I had money, I bought...（应为would buy）
把它当真实条件使用，导致语气不对（真实情况用if+present, will/can）

contrasts.title: if + present, will / can + base
contrasts.note: 用于真实/可能发生的条件：If it rains, we’ll stay home。

contrasts.title: if I were you, I'd...
contrasts.note: 给建议的固定说法，是本句型的常见特例。

contrasts.title: if + present, will / can + base
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: if I were you, I'd...
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: If I had more time, I would learn Chinese every day.
examples.translation: 如果我有更多时间，我就会每天学中文。（但现在时间不够）
examples.note: 现在不真实的假设：had + would learn

examples.sentence: If I were you, I’d talk to the teacher first.
examples.translation: 如果我是你，我会先跟老师谈谈。
examples.note: 建议：were常见于该固定句型

templates.label: Unreal present conditional
slotHints: if从句用一般过去时（表示与现在相反）
主句用would/’d + 动词原形
templates.exampleSentence: If she lived closer, she would visit us more often.
templates.exampleTranslation: 如果她住得近一点，她就会更常来看我们。（但她住得不近）

items.title: if I were you, I'd...
items.pattern: if I were you, I would + base
items.coreExplanation: “If I were you, I’d/ I would + 动词原形”表示“如果我是你，我会……”，用来委婉给建议。were用于所有人称（I/he/she/it）更自然。
items.usageNote: 常用缩写：I’d。语气比“You should”更柔和。

slotSchema.label: 建议做的事
slotSchema.hint: 用动词原形开头，如: talk to..., take a break, see a doctor

commonErrors: 说成 if I was you（更口语但不如 were 标准）
would 后面加 to（误：I would to go）
把主句用 will（误：If I were you, I will...）

contrasts.title: you should + base
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: if + past, would + base
contrasts.note: 泛指假设：If I had time, I’d help.；“If I were you”专用于给对方建议。

contrasts.title: if + past, would + base
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: If I were you, I’d talk to your manager about it.
examples.translation: 如果我是你，我会去跟你的经理谈谈这件事。
examples.note: 给建议，语气委婉。

examples.sentence: If I were you, I’d take a day off and rest.
examples.translation: 如果我是你，我会请一天假好好休息。

templates.label: Basic advice
slotHints: action=动词原形短语：call him back / see a doctor / wait until tomorrow
templates.exampleSentence: If I were you, I’d wait until tomorrow.
templates.exampleTranslation: 如果我是你，我会等到明天。

items.title: as long as + clause
items.pattern: as long as + clause, main clause
items.coreExplanation: “as long as + clause, main clause”表示“只要/只要一直…就…”，强调条件必须持续成立（常用于规则、许可、协议）。
items.usageNote: 可放句首或句中；常与can/will等情态动词连用；用于时间含义时接近“只要…期间”。

slotSchema.label: as long as 后的条件从句
slotSchema.hint: 描述必须持续为真的条件

slotSchema.label: 主句结果/许可/承诺
slotSchema.hint: 说明在条件成立时会发生/允许的事

commonErrors: 把“as long as”误写成“as long”单独使用
用它表达一次性的条件（更像 if），导致语气不自然
与“so long as”（同义）混用但忽略正式度：正式写作更常用 provided that/if

contrasts.title: if + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: provided that + clause
contrasts.note: 更正式、合同/条款感更强，语气更像“前提是…”。

contrasts.title: provided that + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: As long as you follow the rules, you can stay here.
examples.translation: 只要你遵守规则，你就可以留在这里。
examples.note: 强调“遵守规则”需要一直成立。

examples.sentence: I’ll support your plan as long as it doesn’t cost too much.
examples.translation: 只要花费别太高，我就支持你的计划。
examples.note: 常用于协商/条件同意。

templates.label: Permission/Agreement condition
slotHints: condition: 必须持续满足的条件
main: 许可/承诺/结果
templates.exampleSentence: As long as you finish by Friday, you can take Monday off.
templates.exampleTranslation: 只要你周五前完成，你周一就可以休假。

items.title: in case + clause
items.coreExplanation: 用“in case + 从句”表示“以防/万一……”，主句是在为可能发生的情况做准备（预防性措施），不是在说条件一发生就必然导致结果。
items.usageNote: 常与may/might/could等可能性语气搭配。可放句末或句首（句首后常加逗号）。

slotSchema.label: Main clause
slotSchema.hint: 先做的准备/预防动作

slotSchema.label: In-case clause
slotSchema.hint: 可能发生的情况（不用will）

commonErrors: 把in case当成if，用来表示“如果…就…”的直接条件结果
在in case从句里用will（如*in case it will rain）
把in case of（+名词）和in case + 从句混用（如*in case of it rains）

contrasts.title: if + clause
contrasts.note: if强调“条件成立则结果发生”；in case强调“提前准备以防万一”，不表示必然因果。

contrasts.title: unless + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: if + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: Take an umbrella in case it rains.
examples.translation: 带把伞，以防下雨。
examples.note: 主句是准备动作；从句是可能情况。

examples.sentence: I'll save his number in case I need to contact him later.
examples.translation: 我会把他的号码存起来，以防之后需要联系他。

templates.label: Basic
slotHints: 做准备/预防的动作
可能发生的情况（用一般现在时）
templates.exampleSentence: Charge your phone in case you need it.
templates.exampleTranslation: 给手机充电，以防你需要用。

items.title: when + clause
items.coreExplanation: 用 when 引出时间从句，说明“在某个时间点/某种情况发生时”，主句发生。可指一次性事件或一般规律/习惯。
items.usageNote: 从句可在句首或句末；句首常加逗号。描述将来时，when 从句通常用一般现在时表达将来。

slotSchema.label: when 从句
slotSchema.hint: 用一般现在时/一般过去时等描述时间条件，如 when I get home

slotSchema.label: 主句
slotSchema.hint: 描述结果/动作，如 I call my mom

commonErrors: 把 when 从句当作疑问句：误用 When do you arrive, I…（应为 When you arrive, I…）
将来含义时 when 从句误用 will：When I will arrive, I’ll call you（应为 When I arrive…）
把 while 的“持续同时”误用为 when：While I arrived, he left（应为 When I arrived…）

contrasts.title: while + clause (time)
contrasts.note: while 强调两个动作“同时进行/持续期间”；when 更像时间点触发或一般规律。

contrasts.title: once + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: while + clause (time)
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: When I get home, I usually make tea.
examples.translation: 我到家后通常会泡茶。
examples.note: 一般规律/习惯；when 从句用一般现在时。

examples.sentence: When I arrived, he was already waiting.
examples.translation: 我到的时候，他已经在等了。
examples.note: 过去时间点触发；主句可用过去进行/过去完成等。

templates.label: Time trigger with when
slotHints: {whenClause}=时间/条件触发（多用一般现在/一般过去）
{mainClause}=发生的动作/结果
templates.exampleSentence: When you finish, send me the file.
templates.exampleTranslation: 你做完后，把文件发给我。

items.title: while + clause (time)
items.coreExplanation: 用 while 引导时间状语从句，表示两个动作在同一段时间内同时发生（常用于“进行中”的背景动作 + 另一动作）。
items.usageNote: 时间用法通常搭配进行时（was/were + V-ing），强调“在……期间”。不要和“对比转折”的 while 混用。

slotSchema.label: while 从句
slotSchema.hint: 描述背景/持续动作，常用过去进行时或现在进行时

slotSchema.label: 主句
slotSchema.hint: 同时发生的动作，或在这段时间内发生的事件

commonErrors: 把时间用法的 while 误写成转折对比（= although）的意思
while 从句用一般时但想表达“正在进行”，导致时间感不强（如 while I cooked...）
误用 when 替代 while：when 更偏“某一时刻/事件触发”，while 偏“持续期间”

contrasts.title: when + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: while + clause (contrast)
contrasts.note: 对比用法表示“而/但是”，不强调时间同时发生。

contrasts.title: while + clause (contrast)
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: While I was cooking, he was setting the table.
examples.translation: 我做饭的时候，他在摆桌子。
examples.note: 两个动作同时进行，常用过去进行时。

examples.sentence: While we were waiting for the bus, it started to rain.
examples.translation: 我们等公交的时候，开始下雨了。
examples.note: 等待是背景持续动作；“开始下雨”是在这段时间里发生的事件。

templates.label: Time overlap
slotHints: whileClause: 持续进行的背景动作（常用进行时）
mainClause: 同时发生的动作/事件
templates.exampleSentence: While I was studying, my phone kept ringing.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我学习的时候，我的手机一直在响。

items.title: before + clause
items.coreExplanation: 用 before + 从句 表示“在……之前”，说明主句动作发生在从句动作之前。before 后接完整从句（主语+谓语）。
items.usageNote: 常见位置：主句 + before 从句；或 Before 从句, 主句（更强调先做什么）。

slotSchema.label: Main clause
slotSchema.hint: 先发生的动作/结果

slotSchema.label: Before clause
slotSchema.hint: 后发生的动作；用一般现在时表达将来很常见：before you leave

commonErrors: before 后面只放动词原形而不是从句：*before go -> before I go
把时序写反（主句其实发生在之后）导致逻辑不通
误用将来时：*before you will leave（更常用 before you leave）

contrasts.title: after + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: until + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: Please wash your hands before you eat.
examples.translation: 吃饭前请先洗手。
examples.note: 指令场景；主句动作（洗手）发生在从句动作（吃饭）之前。

examples.sentence: I’ll check the schedule before we book the tickets.
examples.translation: 我们订票前我会先查一下时间表。
examples.note: 计划场景；before 从句用一般现在时表示将来。

templates.label: Basic
slotHints: 主句：先做的事
before 从句：后做的事（主语+谓语）
templates.exampleSentence: Finish your homework before you play games.
templates.exampleTranslation: 玩游戏前先把作业写完。

items.title: after + clause
items.coreExplanation: 用“after + 从句”表示两个动作的先后顺序：从句的事先发生，主句的事后发生。可放句首或句中。
items.usageNote: 口语和写作都常用；若强调“立刻在之后”，可用“right after”。

slotSchema.label: after 从句
slotSchema.hint: 用一般过去/现在/完成时均可，按时间选择；从句描述先发生的事

slotSchema.label: 主句
slotSchema.hint: 描述后发生的事

commonErrors: 把时间顺序搞反：after 从句其实是先发生的事
误用成“after to do”：应接从句(主语+谓语)，或改用“after + V-ing”
忽略连接词位置：句首用逗号隔开，句中一般不需要逗号

contrasts.title: before + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: once + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: After I finished the report, I sent it to my manager.
examples.translation: 我写完报告后，把它发给了经理。
examples.note: 句首 after 从句，表示“完成”先发生。

examples.sentence: I felt much better after I talked to her.
examples.translation: 我跟她聊过之后，感觉好多了。
examples.note: 句末 after 从句，更口语自然。

templates.label: Basic
slotHints: afterClause: 先发生的事（完整从句）
mainClause: 后发生的事
templates.exampleSentence: After we arrived, we had dinner.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们到了之后就吃了晚饭。

items.title: until + clause
items.coreExplanation: “until + clause”表示动作/状态持续到某个时间点为止，后面的从句说明终点（某事发生）。
items.usageNote: 常与持续性动词连用（wait, stay, keep, work）。若主句是否定，常可理解为“直到…才…”。

slotSchema.label: Main clause
slotSchema.hint: 描述持续的动作/状态（常用 will/keep/stay/wait）

slotSchema.label: until + clause
slotSchema.hint: 说明结束点：某事发生/某时到来（如 the meeting ends / you come back）

commonErrors: 把until后面接名词时仍用clause（应改为until + noun phrase：until tomorrow / until next week）
用非持续性动词直接配until（如 *I arrive until 5），应改为I won’t arrive until 5 或 I’ll stay until 5
时态不自然：谈将来时，从句一般用一般现在时（I’ll wait until you arrive，不是 *until you will arrive）

contrasts.title: before + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: when + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: I’ll wait here until you get back.
examples.translation: 我会在这里等到你回来。
examples.note: 将来含义：until从句用一般现在时(get back)。

examples.sentence: We didn’t start until everyone arrived.
examples.translation: 直到所有人都到了，我们才开始。
examples.note: 否定+until常表达“直到…才…”。

templates.label: Basic
slotHints: 用持续性动作/状态
用一般现在/一般过去描述终点事件
templates.exampleSentence: I’ll stay at the office until the report is finished.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我会待在办公室，直到报告完成。

items.title: once + clause
items.coreExplanation: “once + clause”表示“一……就……/一旦……就……”。强调前件完成后，主句立刻发生或安排马上执行。
items.usageNote: 常用于计划、指令、流程说明；可指将来或一般情况。

slotSchema.label: once clause
slotSchema.hint: 用一般现在时或现在完成时描述条件/完成的动作

slotSchema.label: main clause
slotSchema.hint: 结果/下一步动作；可用will/imperative

commonErrors: 把once当作“曾经”，误用为过去经历（应为once=as soon as/一旦）
once从句用will（一般不用will：Once you finish..., I’ll...）
与when混用导致语气不清：once更强调“完成后立刻/一旦达成”

contrasts.title: when + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: after + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: Once you finish the report, send it to me.
examples.translation: 你一写完报告就发给我。
examples.note: 指令场景；强调完成后立即执行。

examples.sentence: Once the meeting is over, we’ll go for lunch.
examples.translation: 会议一结束我们就去吃午饭。
examples.note: 计划安排；“is over”表示条件达成。

templates.label: Once condition, then action
slotHints: 在{once_clause}写“条件完成/达成”的动作
在{main_clause}写“立刻发生/下一步”的动作
templates.exampleSentence: Once you get home, call me.
templates.exampleTranslation: 你一到家就给我打电话。

items.title: that-clause after think / say / know
items.coreExplanation: 用于think/say/know/claim等动词后，引出“所想/所说/所知道的内容”。口语中that常可省略，但意思不变。
items.usageNote: 从句用陈述语序；主句是过去时，若内容仍然成立可不回退时态。

slotSchema.label: Reporting verb
slotSchema.hint: 如 think/say/know/believe/claim/hope

slotSchema.label: That-clause content
slotSchema.hint: 完整陈述句，不能用疑问语序

commonErrors: 把that从句写成疑问语序：I think that is he right.
把whether/if问题误用成that：I don't know that he will come.（应为whether/if）
that从句里漏主语或谓语，导致句子不完整

contrasts.title: whether / if + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: wh- clause
contrasts.note: 用于“具体信息问题”：I know what he said.

contrasts.title: wh- clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: I think that we should leave now.
examples.translation: 我觉得我们现在应该走。
examples.note: 口语中that常省略：I think we should leave now.

examples.sentence: She said that the meeting was canceled.
examples.translation: 她说会议取消了。

templates.label: Basic
slotHints: 动词用think/say/know等
从句用陈述句，表达内容/观点/信息
templates.exampleSentence: We know (that) the plan will work.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们知道这个计划会奏效。

items.title: that-clause after fact / idea / belief nouns
items.coreExplanation: 用于抽象名词（fact/idea/belief/claim/assumption等）后，用that从句具体说明“这个名词的内容是什么”。常见结构：the fact/idea/belief/claim + that + 陈述句。
items.usageNote: that可在口语中省略，但正式写作里保留更清晰；从句用陈述语序，不要用疑问倒装。

slotSchema.label: content noun
slotSchema.hint: 抽象名词：the fact/idea/belief/claim/assumption 等

slotSchema.label: that-clause
slotSchema.hint: 陈述句：主语+谓语...；可用现在/过去/完成时

commonErrors: 把that从句写成疑问语序：that is he...（应为that he is...）
用what从句替代导致语义偏移：the fact what...（应为the fact that...）
that从句不完整或缺主语：the idea that can work（应为the idea that it can work / that this plan can work）

contrasts.title: the fact that + clause
contrasts.note: 更强调“事实”本身，常用于论证：The fact that... shows...；而一般noun + that可用于多种名词（idea/claim等）。

contrasts.title: the reason is that + clause
contrasts.note: 用于解释原因的系表结构；不是名词后置内容说明：The reason is that...（“原因是……”）。

contrasts.title: the fact that + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: the reason is that + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: The fact that prices rose so quickly surprised investors.
examples.translation: 价格上涨得如此之快这一事实让投资者感到意外。
examples.note: the fact that + 从句，用来陈述“事实内容”。

examples.sentence: We rejected the assumption that the data was complete.
examples.translation: 我们否定了“数据是完整的”这一假设。
examples.note: assumption后接that从句说明假设的具体内容。

templates.label: State the content of an abstract noun
slotHints: 名词前常加the/this/that等限定词
从句用陈述语序，必要时保留that以更正式清楚
templates.exampleSentence: This belief that hard work always leads to success is widespread.
templates.exampleTranslation: “努力就一定成功”这一信念很普遍。

items.title: what + clause
items.coreExplanation: “what + 从句”表示“……的东西/事情”，相当于名词，可作主语、宾语或表语；从句里what本身充当成分（主语/宾语）。
items.usageNote: 不要再在从句里重复名词（如*what I need it）。若要表达“……这件事”，可用what happened/what you said等。

slotSchema.label: what clause
slotSchema.hint: what + 主语 + 谓语（what在从句中充当主语或宾语）

commonErrors: 把what从句当成疑问句用倒装：*I don't know what is this.
在what从句里重复宾语：*What I need it is time.
用that代替what表达“……的东西”：*I want that you said.

contrasts.title: that-clause after think / say / know
contrasts.note: that从句=“这一事实/观点”；what从句=“……的东西/内容”。I know that he came（我知道他来了）vs I know what he said（我知道他说了什么/他说的内容）。

contrasts.title: wh- clause after ask / know / tell
contrasts.note: what可用于“嵌入式疑问/信息缺口”；但在名词性what从句里，整体指“东西/内容”，常可换成“the thing(s) that…”。

contrasts.title: that-clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: wh- clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: What you said really helped me.
examples.translation: 你说的话真的帮了我。
examples.note: 作主语；=The thing(s) you said…

examples.sentence: I can’t remember what he looks like.
examples.translation: 我记不清他长什么样了。
examples.note: 作宾语；从句语序不倒装。

templates.label: Basic
slotHints: main：主句（如I know / Tell me / What surprised me is…）
templates.exampleSentence: Tell me what you need.
templates.exampleTranslation: 告诉我你需要什么。

items.title: whether / if + clause
items.coreExplanation: 用于引出“是/否”型的间接疑问或不确定的信息：I don’t know whether/if + 陈述语序从句。常接在 know/ask/wonder/decide/check/see/remember 等动词后。
items.usageNote: whether 比 if 更正式；在介词后、to 不定式前、以及 whether...or not 结构中通常用 whether。if 更口语。

slotSchema.label: reporting/knowing verb
slotSchema.hint: 如 know/ask/wonder/decide/check/see

slotSchema.label: yes-no clause
slotSchema.hint: 用陈述语序：whether/if + 主语 + 动词

commonErrors: 把从句写成疑问语序：*I don’t know if is he coming. → if he is coming.
误用 that 引出是/否问题：*I don’t know that he will come. → whether/if he will come.
在介词后用 if：*It depends on if... → It depends on whether...

contrasts.title: that-clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: verb + wh- clause
contrasts.note: wh- 从句用于“信息型”问题（what/where/why），不是 yes/no。

contrasts.title: wh- clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: I’m not sure whether I should take the job.
examples.translation: 我不确定我是否应该接受这份工作。
examples.note: whether 更自然，表达不确定与权衡。

examples.sentence: Could you check if the meeting is still on?
examples.translation: 你能确认一下会议是否还照常进行吗？
examples.note: 口语中常用 if；从句用陈述语序。

templates.label: Uncertainty / indirect yes-no question
slotHints: v: know/ask/wonder/decide/check/see
clause: 主语+动词（陈述语序）
templates.exampleSentence: We need to decide whether we can finish by Friday.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们需要决定是否能在周五前完成。

items.title: wh- clause after ask / know / tell
items.coreExplanation: 用在“ask/know/tell/remember/figure out”等动词后，把疑问词引导的从句当作宾语：I don’t know how to do it / how he did it。注意这是“陈述语序”，不用倒装。
items.usageNote: 常见wh-词：what/where/when/why/how/who/which；后面可接完整从句，也可接“wh- + to do”（当主语一致时更自然）。

slotSchema.label: Verb
slotSchema.hint: ask/know/tell/remember/figure out 等

slotSchema.label: wh- clause
slotSchema.hint: how/what/where/when/why/who/which + (S + V) 或 wh- + to + V

commonErrors: 把embedded question写成疑问语序：I don’t know how does it work.（应为 how it works）
误加问号：Please tell me where you are?（通常不用?）
该用whether/if时误用wh-：I don’t know where he will come.（应为 whether/if he will come，表示“是否来”）

contrasts.title: whether / if + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: what + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: I don’t know how it works.
examples.translation: 我不知道它是怎么运作的。
examples.note: embedded question用陈述语序：how it works。

examples.sentence: Can you tell me where to park?
examples.translation: 你能告诉我在哪里停车吗？
examples.note: 主语一致时可用“where to + V”。

templates.label: Ask/know/tell + wh- clause
slotHints: 例如：I don’t know / Can you tell me / Please ask
例如：how it works / where to park / what she said
templates.exampleSentence: Please tell me when the meeting starts.
templates.exampleTranslation: 请告诉我会议什么时候开始。

items.title: the fact that + clause
items.coreExplanation: “the fact that + 从句”把后面的内容名词化，强调这是一个既定事实或需要被讨论的事实点；常作主语、宾语或介词宾语。
items.usageNote: 常用于较正式的论证/写作；that一般不省略，且后面必须是完整句子（主语+谓语）。

slotSchema.label: that-clause
slotSchema.hint: 用完整陈述句：主语+谓语，如“he was late”

commonErrors: 把that从句写成短语：*the fact that his lateness（应为the fact that he was late）
误用what：*the fact what...（应为the fact that...）
在非正式口语中过度使用，显得生硬（可改用“That...”或“Actually,...”）

contrasts.title: noun + that + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: the reason is that + clause
contrasts.note: 用于解释原因；“the fact that...”只是提出一个事实点，不一定是原因。

contrasts.title: the reason is that + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: The fact that the data were incomplete undermines the conclusion.
examples.translation: 数据不完整这一事实削弱了该结论。
examples.note: 作主语，强调“事实点→影响”。

examples.sentence: We cannot ignore the fact that costs have risen sharply.
examples.translation: 我们不能忽视成本大幅上升这一事实。
examples.note: 作宾语，常与ignore/accept/acknowledge等动词搭配。

templates.label: Raise a fact point
templates.template: We cannot ignore the fact that {clause}.
slotHints: 用完整陈述句，时态与事实一致
templates.exampleSentence: We cannot ignore the fact that customers are leaving.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们不能忽视客户正在流失这一事实。

items.title: it is + adjective + that...
items.coreExplanation: 用 it is + adjective + that + 从句 来对“that 从句中的内容”做评价或表态；it 为形式主语，that 从句是真正内容。常见形容词：clear, important, likely, possible, surprising, unfortunate 等。
items.usageNote: 更常见于书面/正式表达；口语可用 It’s… that… 或改用 I think/It seems…。that 在口语中有时可省略，但正式写作建议保留。

slotSchema.label: Adjective
slotSchema.hint: 如 important/clear/likely/surprising/necessary 等

slotSchema.label: That-clause
slotSchema.hint: 陈述一个观点/事实/判断；可用完整从句

commonErrors: 把 that 从句写成不完整句（缺主语或谓语）
误用 it is + adj + to...：当后面是完整从句时应选 that 从句
形容词搭配不当（如 *it is likely that to...）

contrasts.title: it's + adjective + to...
contrasts.note: to 不定式更像“做某事”本身；that 从句是对“一个命题/事实”评价。例：It’s important to exercise. vs It’s important that you exercise.

contrasts.title: that-clause after think / say / know
contrasts.note: 用主语明确观点来源：I think/say/know that...；it is + adj + that 更客观/更像评价结论。

contrasts.title: it's + adjective + to...
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: that-clause after think / say / know
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: It is important that we act now.
examples.translation: 我们现在就采取行动很重要。
examples.note: 强调“采取行动”这一命题的重要性（较正式）。

examples.sentence: It is clear that the data were misreported.
examples.translation: 很明显数据被错误地报告了。
examples.note: 用于报告/分析中的判断。

templates.label: Make an evaluation
slotHints: 形容词选评价/判断类（important/likely/clear/necessary等）
that 从句用完整陈述句，表达你要评价的内容
templates.exampleSentence: It is likely that prices will rise.
templates.exampleTranslation: 价格很可能会上涨。

items.title: who relative clause
items.coreExplanation: 用来修饰前面的名词（人），在从句里who充当主语：the person who + 动词…（=“那个……的人”）。
items.usageNote: 从句里不要再重复主语（不要再用he/she）。主从句时态按语境决定。

slotSchema.label: Person noun
slotSchema.hint: 要被修饰的人，如 the man/my friend/someone

slotSchema.label: who-clause
slotSchema.hint: 以who开头，后面接谓语；who在从句中作主语

commonErrors: 在who从句里重复主语：*the man who he lives next door
把人用which修饰：*the woman which called me
把关系代词省略：*the person I met yesterday（这里需要who/that作主语）

contrasts.title: that relative clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: whose + noun
contrasts.note: 表示“……的”：the man whose car was stolen（不是who car…）。

contrasts.title: whose + noun
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: She’s the doctor who helped my mom.
examples.translation: 她就是帮助过我妈妈的那位医生。
examples.note: who在从句里作主语（helped的执行者）。

examples.sentence: I met someone who speaks four languages.
examples.translation: 我遇到一个会说四种语言的人。
examples.note: someone后接who从句，补充身份特征。

templates.label: Identify a person
slotHints: noun=要描述的人
whoClause=who后面的动作/状态（不要再加he/she）
templates.exampleSentence: This is the teacher who explains everything clearly.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这就是那位把每件事都讲得很清楚的老师。

items.title: which relative clause
items.coreExplanation: 用“which”引导定语从句来修饰前面的名词，说明“是哪一个/什么样的东西”。常用于事物、观点、事情。
items.usageNote: 可作限定性（不加逗号，指明是哪一个）或非限定性（加逗号，补充说明）。非限定性更常用which，不常用that。

slotSchema.label: Noun (thing/idea)
slotSchema.hint: 被修饰的事物/概念名词

slotSchema.label: Which clause
slotSchema.hint: 从句里需要一个“缺位”对应前面的名词（主语或宾语）

commonErrors: 把非限定性从句写成不加逗号，导致意思变成“限定哪一个”
从句结构不完整：which后面缺谓语或主语宾语关系不清
用which指人（人一般用who/that）

contrasts.title: that relative clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: where relative clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: The report which explains the changes is attached.
examples.translation: 解释这些变化的报告已作为附件发送。
examples.note: 限定性：which从句说明“是哪一份报告”。

examples.sentence: The device, which was released last year, is already sold out.
examples.translation: 这款设备去年发布，如今已经售罄。
examples.note: 非限定性：逗号表示补充信息。

templates.label: Basic
slotHints: 先写要修饰的名词，再用which接从句；从句里与名词对应的位置要“空出来”（如作主语/宾语）
templates.exampleSentence: I chose a plan which fits my budget.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我选了一个符合我预算的方案。

items.title: that relative clause
items.coreExplanation: 用在限定性定语从句：在名词后用 that 引出从句，补充“是哪一个/哪些”的关键信息；可指人或物。
items.usageNote: 多用于口语和一般写作；限定性从句通常不加逗号。that 在从句中作宾语时常可省略。

slotSchema.label: Head noun
slotSchema.hint: 被修饰的名词（人/物）

slotSchema.label: that clause
slotSchema.hint: that + 从句；说明该名词的关键信息

commonErrors: 把限定性定语从句用逗号隔开（*the book, that...）
在 that 作宾语已省略时仍重复宾语（*the book that I bought it）
在人前一律用 who，忽视 that 也可指人（口语常用 that）

contrasts.title: who relative clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: which relative clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: Do you know the guy that lives upstairs?
examples.translation: 你认识住在楼上的那个人吗？
examples.note: that 指人；限定性信息“住在楼上”。

examples.sentence: This is the phone (that) I bought yesterday.
examples.translation: 这是我昨天买的那部手机。
examples.note: that 作宾语可省略。

templates.label: Define a person/thing with that
slotHints: 先给出名词，再用 that + 从句说明“是哪一个/哪些”
templates.exampleSentence: I like movies that make me laugh.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我喜欢那些能让我发笑的电影。

items.title: whose + noun
items.coreExplanation: 用 whose 表示“……的”，在定语从句里连接先行词（人或物）与其所属/拥有的名词。结构：名词 + whose + 名词 + 从句。
items.usageNote: whose 后面必须跟一个名词（如 whose car/whose idea），不能单独用。

slotSchema.label: 先行词名词
slotSchema.hint: 要被说明的人或物

slotSchema.label: 所属名词
slotSchema.hint: 属于先行词的东西/部分

slotSchema.label: 从句内容
slotSchema.hint: 围绕所属名词继续说明

commonErrors: 把 whose 当成疑问词来问“谁的”，导致整句缺少先行词
whose 后面不加名词（误：the man whose is broken）
用 his/her/its 代替 whose，导致结构不自然或指代不清

contrasts.title: who relative clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: that relative clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: I met a woman whose brother works at Google.
examples.translation: 我遇到一位女士，她的哥哥在谷歌工作。
examples.note: whose brother = her brother（更正式/书面、避免重复）。

examples.sentence: We stayed in a hotel whose rooms face the ocean.
examples.translation: 我们住在一家酒店，酒店的房间朝向大海。
examples.note: whose 也可指物/机构的“……的”。

templates.label: Basic
slotHints: antecedent=先行词名词
possessedNoun=所属名词
clause=对所属名词的补充说明
templates.exampleSentence: She's the author whose books changed my life.
templates.exampleTranslation: 她就是那位作者，她的书改变了我的人生。

items.title: where relative clause
items.coreExplanation: 用“place + where + clause”给地点加补充信息；where在从句里表示“在那个地方/在那里”。
items.usageNote: 先用地点名词，再用where引出从句；不要在从句里重复地点（如*where I live in）。

slotSchema.label: Place noun
slotSchema.hint: 地点名词或地点短语，如 the park / the city / the hotel

slotSchema.label: Where-clause
slotSchema.hint: 包含主谓，描述在该地点发生的事；不要再加in/at + the place

commonErrors: 在where从句里重复介词+地点：*the city where I live in
把where当成问句用法，误写成倒装或问号：*the place where do you work?
用which代替where但不补介词：*the place which I work（应为where I work 或 which I work in）

contrasts.title: which relative clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: when relative clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: This is the café where we first met.
examples.translation: 这就是我们第一次见面的那家咖啡馆。
examples.note: where = 在那家咖啡馆里

examples.sentence: I want to visit the town where my grandparents grew up.
examples.translation: 我想去看看我祖父母长大的那个小镇。

templates.label: Basic
slotHints: place=地点名词短语
clause=在该地点发生的事情（陈述句语序）
templates.exampleSentence: Let’s meet at the park where the big fountain is.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们在有大喷泉的那个公园见面吧。

items.title: when relative clause
items.coreExplanation: 用“time + when + clause”给某个时间/时期加补充信息，相当于“在……的时候/那时”。
items.usageNote: 先有时间名词（the day/year/time/period等），再用when引出从句；不是用来问时间的疑问句。

slotSchema.label: Time phrase
slotSchema.hint: 表示时间/时期的名词短语，如 the day, the year, the time, the period

slotSchema.label: When clause
slotSchema.hint: when + 主语 + 动词，描述当时发生的事

commonErrors: 把关系从句误写成疑问句：*the day when did we meet
省略先行词time，直接用when：*I remember when we met（此句可对，但不符合该结构；这里when引导宾语从句）
when从句时态混乱，和时间短语不匹配（如谈过去却用一般现在时）

contrasts.title: where relative clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: why relative clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: I still remember the day when we first met.
examples.translation: 我仍然记得我们第一次见面的那一天。
examples.note: the day是先行词；when从句补充说明是哪一天。

examples.sentence: That was a time when people wrote letters by hand.
examples.translation: 那是一个人们用手写信的时代。
examples.note: time/period常用来讲历史背景。

templates.label: Basic
slotHints: time: the day/the year/the time/the period
whenClause: 主语 + 动词 + 其他（过去经历常用一般过去时）
templates.exampleSentence: It was the year when I moved to Beijing.
templates.exampleTranslation: 那是我搬到北京的那一年。

items.title: why relative clause
items.coreExplanation: 用“reason + why + 从句”把原因名词和具体原因连接起来：the reason why + 主语 + 动词... = “……的原因是因为……”（why引导定语从句，修饰reason）。
items.usageNote: 口语里常简化为“the reason is (that) + 从句”。注意why后面是完整句子，不要再加because。

slotSchema.label: reason (noun phrase)
slotSchema.hint: 常用：the reason / a reason

slotSchema.label: why clause
slotSchema.hint: 完整陈述句：S + V...

commonErrors: 把because和why一起用：the reason why ... is because ...
why后面用不完整结构：the reason why of... / why + 名词短语
缺少冠词或结构混乱：reason why I left（可，但更常见the reason why...）

contrasts.title: the reason is that + clause
contrasts.note: 更口语/直接：The reason is (that) I was sick. 不强调定语从句结构。

contrasts.title: reason for + noun/verb-ing
contrasts.note: 后面接名词或动名词：the reason for leaving / for the delay；不能接完整从句。

contrasts.title: the reason is that + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: that-clause after fact / idea / belief nouns
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: The reason why I’m late is that the train was delayed.
examples.translation: 我迟到的原因是火车晚点了。
examples.note: why引导从句修饰reason；句末用is that引出解释。

examples.sentence: Can you explain the reason why you changed your plan?
examples.translation: 你能解释一下你改变计划的原因吗？
examples.note: reason why用于书面/分析性表达较自然。

templates.label: Explain a reason
slotHints: {reason_np}可用the reason
{why_clause}用完整句子，如 I did it / we failed
templates.exampleSentence: The reason why we failed is (that) we didn’t prepare enough.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们失败的原因是准备不够。

items.title: object relative clause with omitted pronoun
items.coreExplanation: 当关系代词(that/which)在定语从句里作宾语时，常可省略：noun + (that/which) + 主语 + 动词 + …（缺的宾语就是前面的noun）。更口语、更自然。
items.usageNote: 只能省略宾语关系代词；若关系代词作主语则不能省略。

slotSchema.label: Head noun
slotSchema.hint: 被修饰的名词/名词短语

slotSchema.label: Relative clause (object omitted)
slotSchema.hint: 从句内要有主语+谓语，但宾语位置留空（指代headNoun）

commonErrors: 把作主语的关系代词也省略：The man ∅ called me（应为who/that called me）
省略后又在从句里加一个代词重复：the book I bought it（应删it）
用错结构把缺口放错位置：the person I spoke was nice（应为I spoke to / spoke with）

contrasts.title: that relative clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: Subject relative clause (cannot omit)
contrasts.note: 关系代词作主语不可省略：the person who called me（不能说the person ∅ called me）。

contrasts.title: which relative clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: This is the book I told you about.
examples.translation: 这就是我跟你提过的那本书。
examples.note: about后面宾语省略=which/that

examples.sentence: Do you remember the restaurant we went to last week?
examples.translation: 你还记得我们上周去的那家餐厅吗？
examples.note: to后面宾语省略=which/that

templates.label: Basic
slotHints: 先写被修饰的名词短语
从句里让它当宾语：I saw ∅ / we talked about ∅ / I sent ∅ to you
templates.exampleSentence: I like the songs you recommended.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我喜欢你推荐的那些歌。

items.title: so that + clause
items.coreExplanation: “so that + 从句”表示目的：做前一件事，是为了让后面的情况发生/能做到某事。后面常搭配情态动词（can/could/will/would/may/might/should）或出现不同主语。
items.usageNote: 口语和书面都常用；注意与结果含义的“so + 结果从句”区分。

slotSchema.label: Main clause
slotSchema.hint: 先说采取的行动/安排

slotSchema.label: so that clause
slotSchema.hint: 目的；常用情态动词或不同主语

commonErrors: 把“so that”误写成“so”而丢失目的含义（导致变成结果）
在“so that”后用不完整的短语而不是完整从句（缺主语/谓语）
时态/情态不搭：表达将来目的却用一般过去且无情态动词

contrasts.title: in order to + base
contrasts.note: 更偏“为了去做某事”，后面接动词原形；若需要不同主语或情态（让别人能…），用“so that + 从句”更自然。

contrasts.title: so + result clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: in order to + base
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: Speak slowly so that everyone can follow.
examples.translation: 说慢一点，好让大家都能跟上。
examples.note: 目的；so that 后用 can 表示“能够”。

examples.sentence: I’ll send you the file now so that you can review it before the meeting.
examples.translation: 我现在把文件发给你，这样你就能在开会前先看一遍。
examples.note: 常见搭配：so that + you can...

templates.label: Purpose with ability
slotHints: 先做的行动/安排
受益者/执行者
想实现的动作
动作的对象（可省略）
templates.exampleSentence: Turn on captions so that your mom can understand the video.
templates.exampleTranslation: 打开字幕，好让你妈妈能看懂这个视频。

items.title: in order to + base
items.pattern: main clause in order to + base
items.coreExplanation: “in order to + base”放在主句后，表示“为了……”，比直接用“to + base”更强调目的、语气更正式。
items.usageNote: 后面接动词原形；否定用“in order not to + base”。口语里常简化为“to + base”。

slotSchema.label: Main clause
slotSchema.hint: 先说做了什么/要做什么

slotSchema.label: Purpose (base verb)
slotSchema.hint: 用“in order to + 动词原形”表达目的

commonErrors: 把后面动词用成过去式/ing（应为动词原形）
用“for + V-ing”直接替换（表达方式不同，易不地道）
把“in order to”误写成“in order that + base”（that后应接从句）

contrasts.title: to + base
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: so that + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: Please read the safety instructions carefully in order to avoid accidents.
examples.translation: 为了避免事故，请仔细阅读安全说明。
examples.note: 用于说明“做A的目的在于B”，语气正式。

examples.sentence: I left early in order to catch the first train.
examples.translation: 我早走是为了赶上第一班火车。

templates.label: Purpose (formal)
slotHints: 先写主句（动作/决定）
写目的：动词原形短语
templates.exampleSentence: We updated the software in order to improve security.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们更新了软件，以提高安全性。

items.title: too + adjective + to...
items.coreExplanation: 用来表示“程度太……以至于不能……”。常带有结果是做不到/不适合做某事的含义。
items.usageNote: too后接形容词，再接to do；否定含义通常来自too本身，不需要再加not。

slotSchema.label: adjective
slotSchema.hint: 描述程度的形容词，如 expensive/busy/tired

slotSchema.label: to + base verb
slotSchema.hint: 要做但做不到的动作，如 to buy/to go/to finish

commonErrors: 把否定重复：too ... to not ...（多数情况下不需要not）
形容词位置错误：too to + adj + verb
用to后加动名词：too adj to doing

contrasts.title: adjective + enough to...
contrasts.note: enough表示“足够……可以……”（能做到）；too表示“太……以至于不能……”（做不到）。

contrasts.title: so + adjective + that...
contrasts.note: so...that后面接从句说明结果；too...to后面用不定式，更简洁。

contrasts.title: adjective + enough to...
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: so + adjective + that...
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: It’s too expensive to buy.
examples.translation: 太贵了，买不起。
examples.note: too=贵到无法买。

examples.sentence: I’m too tired to go out tonight.
examples.translation: 我太累了，今晚不想/不能出去。
examples.note: 常用于解释拒绝或限制。

templates.label: Too + adjective + to...
slotHints: subject可用it/this/I等
action用动词原形，前面带to
templates.exampleSentence: This coffee is too hot to drink.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这咖啡太烫了，没法喝。

items.title: adjective + enough to...
items.coreExplanation: 用于表示“程度足够，从而可以做某事”。结构：be/feel/look + adjective + enough to + 动词原形。
items.usageNote: 常和能力、距离、时间、年龄等形容词搭配；否定用 not + adjective + enough to 表示“不够……所以不能……”。

slotSchema.label: adjective
slotSchema.hint: 表示程度/能力的形容词，如 strong/old/close/quiet

slotSchema.label: base verb
slotSchema.hint: 动词原形，如 lift/drive/understand

commonErrors: 把 enough 放在形容词前：enough old（应为 old enough）
to 后用动名词：to lifting（应为 to lift）
与 too...to 混用导致意思相反：too tired enough to...

contrasts.title: too + adjective + to...
contrasts.note: too...to 表示“太……而不能……”，通常结果是做不了；adj + enough to 表示“够……可以……”。

contrasts.title: enough + noun
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: too + adjective + to...
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: I’m old enough to drive.
examples.translation: 我年龄够大，可以开车了。
examples.note: old enough 表示“年龄达到要求”。

examples.sentence: The room is quiet enough to study in.
examples.translation: 这个房间够安静，可以在里面学习。
examples.note: 可用 to study (in it)；in 放句末更自然。

templates.label: Basic
slotHints: subject=人/物
be=am/is/are/was/were
adj=形容词
action=动词原形
templates.exampleSentence: She is strong enough to carry the box.
templates.exampleTranslation: 她力气够大，能搬这个箱子。

items.title: rather than
items.coreExplanation: “rather than”用来做简洁对比，表示“选择A而不是B/与其B不如A”。A和B在语法形式上尽量保持一致（名词对名词、动词对动词等）。
items.usageNote: 可放在句中连接两个并列选项；B不一定是完全否定，只是偏好较弱。

slotSchema.label: Preferred option (A)
slotSchema.hint: 你更想要/更常做的选项；形式尽量与B一致

slotSchema.label: Less preferred option (B)
slotSchema.hint: 你不那么想要/不那么推荐的选项；常与A平行

commonErrors: A和B形式不平行：如A用动词原形，B用名词/从句导致不自然
把“rather than”误当作“instead”单独使用：应连接两项（A rather than B）
在否定句中逻辑混乱：如“don’t…rather than…”导致不清楚偏好对象

contrasts.title: instead of + noun / v-ing
contrasts.note: “instead of”更像介词短语，常接名词/动名词；“rather than”更常用于并列对比，语气更像“与其…不如…”

contrasts.title: prefer A to B
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: instead of + noun / v-ing
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: I’d call her rather than send a long email.
examples.translation: 我宁愿给她打电话，也不想发一封很长的邮件。
examples.note: A和B都是动词原形（call / send），结构平行。

examples.sentence: We need action rather than more discussion.
examples.translation: 我们需要行动，而不是更多讨论。
examples.note: A和B都是名词，适合写作/论证。

templates.label: Choose A, not B
slotHints: A：更偏好的选项（名词/动词短语等）
B：较不偏好的选项，形式尽量与A一致
templates.exampleSentence: Take the train rather than drive.
templates.exampleTranslation: 坐火车而不是开车。

items.title: instead of + noun / v-ing
items.coreExplanation: “instead of + 名词/动名词”表示“用A代替B；不做B而做A”。常放在句首或句末。
items.usageNote: 动词要用v-ing（doing），不是to do；作主语相同时更自然。

slotSchema.label: 替代选项A
slotSchema.hint: 被选择/被做的东西（名词或v-ing短语）

slotSchema.label: 被替代选项B
slotSchema.hint: 原本的选择/动作（名词或v-ing短语）

commonErrors: 把v-ing写成to do：instead of to go → instead of going
A/B位置颠倒导致意思反了：instead of A, do B（应确认谁替代谁）
后面直接接完整句但缺少改写：instead of I went...（应改为instead of going...或用instead of + clause较复杂结构）

contrasts.title: rather than
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: instead
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: Let’s take the subway instead of driving.
examples.translation: 我们坐地铁吧，别开车了。
examples.note: instead of后用v-ing。

examples.sentence: Instead of coffee, I’ll have tea.
examples.translation: 不喝咖啡，我要喝茶。
examples.note: 名词替换名词，常见点单/选择场景。

templates.label: Suggest an alternative
slotHints: replacement: 选更好的做法（v-ing或名词短语）
original: 原本打算的做法（v-ing或名词短语）
templates.exampleSentence: Let’s meet online instead of traveling there.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们线上见吧，别专门过去了。

items.title: prefer A to B
items.coreExplanation: 用来比较两者并表达偏好：prefer A to B = “比起B更喜欢A”。A和B需同类（名词对名词；动名词对动名词）。
items.usageNote: 表示较稳定/一般性的喜好；谈具体一次性的选择也可用，但更常见用would rather。

slotSchema.label: Option A
slotSchema.hint: 与B保持同一语法类型（名词/动名词）

slotSchema.label: Option B
slotSchema.hint: 用to引出对比对象，不是不定式to

commonErrors: 把to误当作不定式：prefer to do A to do B（应为prefer doing A to doing B 或 prefer to do A rather than do B）
A和B类型不一致：prefer coffee to drinking tea（应都为名词或都为动名词）
用than代替to：prefer A than B（应为prefer A to B）

contrasts.title: rather than
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: would rather
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: I prefer tea to coffee.
examples.translation: 比起咖啡，我更喜欢茶。
examples.note: 名词对名词。

examples.sentence: She prefers working from home to commuting every day.
examples.translation: 她更喜欢在家工作，而不是每天通勤。
examples.note: 动名词对动名词。

templates.label: Basic
slotHints: A与B保持同类：名词/动名词
用to连接比较对象
templates.exampleSentence: I prefer reading to watching TV.
templates.exampleTranslation: 比起看电视，我更喜欢阅读。

items.title: it's + adjective + to...
items.coreExplanation: 用 it 作形式主语，对后面的动作(to do)作评价：It’s + 形容词 + to do...（做某事很/是……）。真正的动作放在句末，更自然。
items.usageNote: 常用形容词：important/necessary/helpful/easy/difficult/safe/wise。也可用 It’s not + adj + to... 表否定评价。

slotSchema.label: adjective
slotSchema.hint: 描述评价：important/necessary/easy/difficult等

slotSchema.label: to + base verb
slotSchema.hint: 要评价的动作：to + 动词原形

commonErrors: 把动词写成过去式/三单：It’s important to learns...（应为 to learn）
误用人作主语导致不自然：I’m important to...（通常用 It’s important to...）
把 to 省略：It’s important learn...（应为 to learn）

contrasts.title: it is + adjective + that...
contrasts.note: that 后接完整从句，强调“某事实/情况”很……：It’s important that you arrive on time.

contrasts.title: too + adjective + to...
contrasts.note: 表示“太……以至于不能……”：too tired to work（不是一般评价）。

contrasts.title: it is + adjective + that...
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: too + adjective + to...
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: It’s important to back up your files.
examples.translation: 备份你的文件很重要。
examples.note: 对动作“备份”作评价。

examples.sentence: It’s difficult to explain this in one sentence.
examples.translation: 用一句话解释这个很难。

templates.label: Evaluate an action
slotHints: 填评价形容词（important/easy/necessary等）
填 to + 动词原形短语
templates.exampleSentence: It’s necessary to wear a seat belt.
templates.exampleTranslation: 系安全带是必要的。

items.title: there is / there are
items.coreExplanation: 用 there is / there are 来表示“有/存在……”，常用于第一次提到某人某物或描述某处有什么。单数/不可数用 there is；复数用 there are。
items.usageNote: 口语里 there are 常缩成 there're；疑问句把 is/are 提前：Is there…?/Are there…?；否定：There isn't/aren't…

slotSchema.label: is/are
slotSchema.hint: 单数/不可数用 is；复数用 are

slotSchema.label: noun
slotSchema.hint: 要介绍的“人/物/情况”

commonErrors: 把 there is/are 误写成 their is/are
主谓不一致：*there are a book
用 it is 代替存在句：*it is a café near here（应为 there is）

contrasts.title: have
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: there is no need to...
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: There is a café near here.
examples.translation: 这附近有一家咖啡馆。
examples.note: 首次引入信息，用 there is 更自然。

examples.sentence: There are three meetings today.
examples.translation: 今天有三个会议。
examples.note: 复数名词用 there are。

templates.label: Basic
slotHints: is/are
a/an + 单数名词 / 不可数名词 / 复数名词
templates.exampleSentence: There are some questions.
templates.exampleTranslation: 有一些问题。

items.title: there is no need to...
items.pattern: there is no need to + base
items.coreExplanation: 用来礼貌地表示“没必要做某事/无需……”，语气较温和，常用于建议或安抚。
items.usageNote: 后面接动词原形；可加 for + 名词/代词 指明对象（如 for you）。

slotSchema.label: action (base verb phrase)
slotSchema.hint: 动词原形开头，如 worry / rush / book a taxi

commonErrors: 把动词写成 v-ing：there is no need to worrying（应为 worry）
漏掉 to：there is no need worry（应为 to worry）
把 need 当名词时误用：there is no need of to...（应为 no need to... 或 no need for...）

contrasts.title: you don't have to...
contrasts.note: 更口语、更直接，强调“你不必”。there is no need to 更委婉、较书面。

contrasts.title: there is no point in...
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: you don't have to...
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: There is no need to worry about the deadline; we have plenty of time.
examples.translation: 没必要担心截止日期；我们时间很充足。
examples.note: 用于安抚对方情绪。

examples.sentence: There is no need to include personal details in the report.
examples.translation: 报告里没必要写个人信息。
examples.note: 较正式写法，适合邮件/报告。

templates.label: No need (general)
slotHints: action: 动词原形短语，如 bring cash / explain again / rush
templates.exampleSentence: There is no need to bring cash.
templates.exampleTranslation: 没必要带现金。

items.title: the reason is that...
items.coreExplanation: 用来把“原因”明确引出来：先说“the reason”，再用that引导原因从句，语气清晰、偏书面。
items.usageNote: that后的从句要完整（有主语+谓语）。口语中也可省略that，但正式写作建议保留。

slotSchema.label: 原因从句
slotSchema.hint: 完整陈述句，说明原因

commonErrors: 用because替换that：*the reason is because...（常被认为不规范）
that后用不完整成分：*the reason is that the delay（缺谓语）
把结果当原因写进that从句，导致逻辑反了

contrasts.title: that's why + result
contrasts.note: “the reason is that”强调原因；“that's why”强调结果/结论。

contrasts.title: the fact that + clause
contrasts.note: “the reason is that”回答“为什么”；“the fact that”强调“事实本身”，不一定是原因。

contrasts.title: that's why + result
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: the fact that + clause
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: The reason is that we don’t have enough data yet.
examples.translation: 原因是我们目前还没有足够的数据。
examples.note: 用于解释原因，语气中性偏书面。

examples.sentence: The reason is that the server was down for maintenance.
examples.translation: 原因是服务器在维护期间宕机了。

templates.label: State the reason clearly
slotHints: 用完整句子说明原因（主语+谓语）
templates.exampleSentence: The reason is that I forgot to set an alarm.
templates.exampleTranslation: 原因是我忘了设闹钟。

items.title: how about + v-ing
items.coreExplanation: 用“how about + 动名词/名词”来提出建议、给出备选方案或转移到另一个选项，语气轻松。
items.usageNote: 常用于口语对话；也可用于回应对方的计划：提出替代方案。

slotSchema.label: 建议/选项
slotSchema.hint: 用动名词(v-ing)或名词短语：going for a walk / pizza

commonErrors: 误用不定式：how about to go（应为 how about going）
只说“how about”不补全内容，导致信息不完整
用在过于正式的邮件/报告里，语气显得随意

contrasts.title: why don't we + base
contrasts.note: 更直接地提议一起做某事，语气更强一点。

contrasts.title: would you like to...
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: why don't we...
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: How about getting some coffee after the meeting?
examples.translation: 会议结束后去喝杯咖啡怎么样？
examples.note: how about + v-ing，用于提出轻松建议。

examples.sentence: We’re out of time—how about Plan B?
examples.translation: 我们没时间了——方案B怎么样？
examples.note: how about + 名词，快速抛出备选项。

templates.label: Casual suggestion
slotHints: {idea} 填 v-ing 或名词短语，如: taking a taxi / lunch at noon / another time
templates.exampleSentence: How about taking a taxi?
templates.exampleTranslation: 坐出租车怎么样？

items.title: would you like to...
items.coreExplanation: “Would you like to + 动词原形”用于礼貌地邀请或提出建议/提供帮助，比“Do you want to…?”更客气。
items.usageNote: 常用于服务场景或正式一点的对话；回答可用“Yes, I'd love to./Sure.”或“No, thanks.”

slotSchema.label: 想做的动作
slotSchema.hint: to + 动词原形，如 to join us / to have a seat

commonErrors: 把动词原形写成动名词：Would you like to going...?（应为 to go）
用want替换导致语气变硬：Do you want to...?（不够礼貌）
漏掉to：Would you like join us?（应为 like to join）

contrasts.title: do you want to...
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: how about + v-ing
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: Would you like to join us for dinner?
examples.translation: 你想和我们一起吃晚饭吗？（礼貌邀请）

examples.sentence: Would you like to have a seat?
examples.translation: 您想坐一下吗？（礼貌提供）
examples.note: 服务/接待场景常用。

templates.label: Polite invitation/offer
slotHints: {action}: to + 动词原形（to do...）
templates.exampleSentence: Would you like to take a look?
templates.exampleTranslation: 您想看一下吗？

items.title: make / let / help + object + do
items.coreExplanation: 用于表达“使/让/帮”某人做某事：make 表示强迫或要求；let 表示允许；help 表示帮助。三者后面常用“宾语 + 动词原形”。
items.usageNote: help 后可用动词原形或 to do（更完整/更正式一点），但本卡片聚焦“宾语+原形”。被动语态中 make 通常变为 be made to do。

slotSchema.label: Causative verb
slotSchema.hint: make / let / help

slotSchema.label: Object (person)
slotSchema.hint: me / him / my mom / the team

slotSchema.label: Base verb
slotSchema.hint: 动词原形：do / go / clean / finish

commonErrors: 把动词写成 to do：make/let 后一般不用 to
把动词加 -s/-ed：这里要用动词原形
把“让/使”都用 let：表达“逼/要求”更常用 make

contrasts.title: ask someone to...
contrasts.note: ask sb to do 表示请求/请某人做；语气更礼貌，不强调强制或允许。

contrasts.title: tell someone to...
contrasts.note: tell sb to do 表示命令/通知某人做；更像“说/告诉他去做”，不强调“使他做到”。

contrasts.title: ask someone to...
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: tell someone to...
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: My boss made me stay late.
examples.translation: 老板让我加班到很晚。
examples.note: make 表示要求/强制。

examples.sentence: Can you help me carry this?
examples.translation: 你能帮我搬一下这个吗？
examples.note: help + 宾语 + 原形，口语常见。

templates.label: Basic
slotHints: verb=make/let/help
object=人
baseVerb=动词原形
templates.exampleSentence: Please let me explain.
templates.exampleTranslation: 请让我解释一下。

items.title: have trouble + v-ing
items.coreExplanation: 用“have trouble + v-ing”表示做某事有困难/不顺利，强调过程中的阻碍；trouble后接动名词。
items.usageNote: 更自然的说法常用have trouble/difficulty (in) doing；若指某次具体困难也可用had trouble。

slotSchema.label: Subject
slotSchema.hint: 人/物作主语，如 I / she / my phone

slotSchema.label: Activity
slotSchema.hint: 动名词，如 sleeping / understanding / logging in

slotSchema.label: Context
slotSchema.hint: 可选：加in/with/when等短语说明情境

commonErrors: trouble后用to do：have trouble to understand（应为understanding）
把trouble当可数：have a trouble（应为have trouble / have a problem）
漏掉动名词的宾语：have trouble finding（不说清楚finding what）

contrasts.title: can't + base
contrasts.note: can't更像“做不到/不能”，语气更绝对；have trouble表示“能做但很难/不顺”。

contrasts.title: be good at + v-ing
contrasts.note: be good at强调擅长；have trouble强调不擅长或遇到障碍。

contrasts.title: can't + base
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: be good at + v-ing
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: I have trouble falling asleep at night.
examples.translation: 我晚上很难入睡。
examples.note: fall asleep用动名词falling。

examples.sentence: She had trouble understanding the instructions.
examples.translation: 她理解说明书时遇到了困难。
examples.note: 过去具体情境用had trouble。

templates.label: Basic
slotHints: subject=人/物
activity=动名词短语
context=可选的情境补充，如 at night / with + 名词 / when + 从句
templates.exampleSentence: We have trouble logging in on this Wi‑Fi.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们连这个Wi‑Fi时登录很困难。

items.title: not only... but also...
items.pattern: not only A but also B
items.coreExplanation: 用来强调“除了A之外还有B”，结构对称，语气更有力度，常用于写作和论证。
items.usageNote: 可用于名词、动词、形容词、句子等并列；正式写作中常见。注意：当not only放句首时，后面常用倒装（Not only did I..., but I also...）。

slotSchema.label: A（第一点）
slotSchema.hint: 与B保持同类：名词对名词/动词对动词/句子对句子

slotSchema.label: B（第二点）
slotSchema.hint: 更想强调的补充点；可加also加强

slotSchema.label: 主语/语境（可选）
slotSchema.hint: 需要时补齐主语或谓语，使句子完整

commonErrors: A和B不平行（例如A是名词，B是从句），导致结构不对称
not only放句首却不倒装（尤其是带助动词时）
重复使用also太多或位置混乱（常用：but also + B）

contrasts.title: both A and B
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: also
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: Not only is this plan cheaper, but it’s also faster.
examples.translation: 这个方案不仅更便宜，而且也更快。
examples.note: 形容词并列（cheaper/faster），语气强调；not only句首触发倒装is this plan。

examples.sentence: She not only speaks English but also writes it well.
examples.translation: 她不仅会说英语，而且写得也很好。
examples.note: 动词短语并列（speaks/writes）；not only不在句首，不需要倒装。

templates.label: Balanced emphasis
slotHints: A放在not only后，可为句子/短语；若A为完整分句且not only在句首，注意倒装
B与A保持平行结构，可在but后加also
templates.exampleSentence: Not only did we finish on time, but we also improved the quality.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们不仅按时完成了，而且还提升了质量。

items.title: the more..., the more...
items.pattern: the more X, the more Y
items.coreExplanation: 用来表达“一个因素变化得越多/越强，另一个结果也随之越多/越强”的相关递进关系。两个分句通常都用比较级。
items.usageNote: 常用逗号分开两部分；两边结构尽量对称。可用 more/less/older/cheaper 等比较级。

slotSchema.label: X（变化因素）
slotSchema.hint: 比较级结构，如: more you practice / harder it gets / older I get

slotSchema.label: Y（随之变化的结果）
slotSchema.hint: 比较级结构，如: better you become / more I realize / less I sleep

commonErrors: 把比较级写成原级：the more you practice, the good you are（应为 the better）
两边结构不对称或缺主语：the more practice, the better you become（更自然：the more you practice...）
误用 because/so 把它当因果连词：Because the more..., so the more...（不需要）

contrasts.title: as...as...
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: comparative sentences
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: The more you practice, the better you get.
examples.translation: 你练得越多，你就越熟练。
examples.note: 两边都用比较级：more / better。

examples.sentence: The harder I work, the less time I have for hobbies.
examples.translation: 我工作越努力，留给爱好的时间就越少。
examples.note: 第二分句可以用 less 表示“越……越少”。

templates.label: Basic
slotHints: X：包含比较级的分句（如 more/less/older/faster + 主语 + 动词）
Y：包含比较级的分句（如 more/less/better/worse + 主语 + 动词）
templates.exampleSentence: The more I read, the more I understand.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我读得越多，就理解得越多。

items.title: it is / was ... that ...
items.pattern: it is / was + focus + that + clause
items.coreExplanation: 用分裂句把句子某一部分提到前面，突出“真正要强调的是focus”。原句信息不变，只是把焦点说得更明确。
items.usageNote: 常用于纠正、对比或正式表达；that后面必须是完整从句。focus可为人/物/时间/地点/原因等。

slotSchema.label: Focus (强调部分)
slotSchema.hint: 可为人/物/时间/地点等；也可用介词短语如 on Friday / in Paris

slotSchema.label: That-clause (其余信息)
slotSchema.hint: 用完整陈述句结构；focus在原句中对应的位置被“挖空”

commonErrors: 把that后面写成不完整句（缺主语或谓语）
误用because：应说 It is ... that ...，不是 It is ... because ...（除非强调原因且focus为the reason）
focus与that从句重复（同一成分出现两次）

contrasts.title: the reason is that...
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

contrasts.title: I think / say / know that...
contrasts.note: 这是表达观点/陈述内容的that从句，不是强调结构；it is...that...是在做“焦点突出/纠正”。

contrasts.title: that-clause after think / say / know
contrasts.note: Easily confused with this pattern; compare scenario and slot usage carefully.

examples.sentence: It was Sarah that solved the problem, not me.
examples.translation: 解决这个问题的是Sarah，不是我。
examples.note: 用was对应过去；后面加not me做纠正对比更自然。

examples.sentence: It’s on Friday that we’re meeting, not Thursday.
examples.translation: 我们是周五见面，不是周四。
examples.note: 强调时间时focus可用介词短语。

templates.label: Emphasize the focus
slotHints: focus放你要强调的信息（人/事/时间/地点等）
that后用完整从句，把其余信息说完
templates.exampleSentence: It is this detail that matters most.
templates.exampleTranslation: 最重要的是这个细节。
